name: Swimlane Task Bar
description: Canvas-rendered task bar and hover tooltip for timed events in swimlane and timeline surfaces. Draws an IN/compute/OUT three-segment bar when the task carries raw input/output arrays; otherwise falls back to a single solid compute bar (no IN/OUT chips or side tints).

source.visualContract: pypto-swimlane-perf-tool/js/swimlane.js task-bar render block and PMU swimlane .sl-tooltip hover behavior

useWhen: Displaying a timed task in a swimlane, timeline, or execution trace surface.
Showing input, compute, and output subsegments inside one task duration when raw input/output arrays are present.
Drawing a single solid bar (no IN/OUT subsegments) for events that have no raw input/output arrays, such as pipeline forward/backward/communication/bubble timeline events.
Showing a task hover tooltip with op, lane, timing, status, dominant counter, and wrap metadata.
rules: Three-segment mode is opt-in via the data: IN and OUT segments are drawn only when task.inputRawMagic / task.outputRawMagic are non-empty arrays. When both are absent or empty, drawTaskBar renders one solid compute bar and omits the IN/OUT chips and side tints. Do not synthesize empty input/output arrays just to force the segmented style on events that have no in/out semantics.
forbiddenOverrides: DOM/CSS reimplementation of the task bar
agentReuseRule: Import patterns/swimlane-task/pattern.js and call window.PtoSwimlaneTaskPattern.drawTaskBar for the canvas bar, createTaskColormap for semantic/stitch/engine/subgraph colors, and initHoverTooltip for hover tips. Do not rebuild the task bar or tooltip behavior with page-local DOM/CSS or rewrite the segment math, color hashing, colormap selection, or hover tip behavior locally.
